entries.note: Implemented feat-workflow-rename (bug A1). Changed src/server/scheduler/ingest.ts to append the first 8 characters of the workflow UUID to the project name at ingest time, producing names like 'yoke-abc12345' instead of 'yoke'. Updated the resume-path SELECT query to match both old-format names (exact match) and new-format names (LIKE prefix-%) so existing live workflows still resume correctly. Created tests/scheduler/ingest.test.ts with 3 tests: (1) name format matches /^${projectName}-[0-9a-f]{8}$/, (2) two consecutive fresh ingests produce distinct names, (3) resume path returns the same workflowId without changing the name. All 53 scheduler tests pass with no regressions.
known_risks: The resume-path LIKE pattern '${projectName}-%' could match an unrelated workflow if two project names share a prefix (e.g. 'yoke' and 'yoke-extended'). In practice this is unlikely since configDir is also checked, but it's a theoretical edge case.
The PATCH /api/workflows/:id rename endpoint (listed as 'bonus scope' in the spec) was intentionally omitted — the spec says to leave it out if unclear, and it would require UI wiring to ship safely.

entries.note: Implemented both integration test files for test-api-control-and-attention. (1) tests/api/control-idempotency.test.ts already existed with 3 passing tests covering HTTP-only idempotency, WS-only idempotency, and cross-transport idempotency. The cross-transport test was strengthened to capture the WS cached response and assert byte-for-byte equality against the original HTTP response body, satisfying RC-3. All 3 tests pass using a real Fastify instance, real SQLite, and a real ws client connection with a counting wrapper around makeControlExecutor to verify single execution. (2) tests/notifications/attention-roundtrip.test.ts is newly created with 5 tests covering: snapshot includes pending attention entry; ack clears the row and broadcasts workflow.index.update with count=0; second ack returns already_acknowledged with no second broadcast; acking one of two items decrements the broadcast count to 1; GET /api/workflows still returns the workflow after ack. The ackAttention callback is wired to call registry.broadcast('workflow.index.update', ...) after clearing acknowledged_at, enabling WS assertions. All 8 tests pass.
known_risks: Cross-transport byte-for-byte equality (RC-3): the WS echoes back the raw HTTP JSON body (not a proper ServerFrame), so the WS frame is structurally different from a normal notice frame. The idempotency store holds whatever was set first, which for HTTP→WS means the WS receives the HTTP body directly. This is the current protocol behavior; if it changes, the assertion will break.

The attention-roundtrip 'second ack no broadcast' test relies on a deliberate timing gap — it closes the WS socket after consuming the first broadcast and does not wait for a second message. If the server emits a second broadcast due to a bug, the test will not catch it (it only catches it indirectly via the closed socket).
The wfSeq counter in attention-roundtrip.test.ts is module-level, so parallel test runs in the same process could produce duplicate IDs. Currently tests run serially per file, so this is safe.

entries.note: feat-workflow-archive is fully implemented. Three pieces landed as specified: (1) Migration 0003_workflow_archive.sql adds archived_at TEXT column to workflows with a partial index on (archived_at IS NULL) for the default list query. (2) API: archive-workflow.ts implements makeArchiveWorkflowFn (RC-3 injection pattern matching ackAttention/retryItems); server.ts adds POST /api/workflows/:id/archive and POST /api/workflows/:id/unarchive (both delegate writes to the injected callback), and updates GET /api/workflows to exclude archived rows by default with ?archived=true to include them; start.ts wires the callback. (3) UI: WorkflowList.tsx adds an archive icon button on row hover that POSTs to archive/unarchive and optimistically removes the row on success; the Show archived checkbox now sends ?archived=true and actually filters. Also fixed two pre-existing bugs discovered during build: @shared alias in vite.config.ts and tsconfig.json pointed to a non-existent repo-root shared/ directory (corrected to src/shared/); WorkflowIndexUpdatePayload.status typed as string instead of WorkflowStatus (corrected). Tests: 10 Vitest API tests cover all 5 AC paths (archive 200, unarchive 200, 409 on in_progress, 404 unknown, default excludes archived, ?archived=true includes only archived). 4 Playwright tests at src/web/e2e/real/workflow-archive.spec.ts cover archive button hover/click, 409 no-crash, Show archived checkbox, unarchive removal. Full suite: 108 Vitest API tests pass, 168 Playwright e2e tests pass (no regressions).
deferred_criteria: src/web/e2e/real/workflow-archive.spec.ts uses page.route() mocks rather than a real SQLite backend — the realBackend fixture (test-real-backend-fixture) has not been implemented yet. The Playwright spec exercises the full UI round-trip (hover, click, checkbox) against mocked API responses. When test-real-backend-fixture lands, this spec should be updated to seed workflows via db.writer and assert against a live server.
known_risks: The archive button uses group-hover (Tailwind CSS group class) to appear on row hover. If the outer div does not correctly inherit group hover state in all browsers, the button may remain invisible. Tested in Chromium only.

Optimistic row removal on archive success: if the POST /archive returns 200 but the server-side broadcast fails to reach connected WS clients, the sidebar row is removed from the client view but subsequent page reloads will restore it. This is consistent with the pattern used by retry/ack-attention.

The 409 conflict check in makeArchiveWorkflowFn reads workflow status then updates in a single sequential pair of prepared statements on the writer connection — not wrapped in a transaction. Under concurrent scheduler activity, an in_progress workflow could theoretically transition away between the read and the 409 check. The window is narrow and acceptable given SQLite's serialized write semantics.

entries.note: Implemented test-state-machine-coverage in full. Added EVENT_VALUES to src/server/state-machine/states.ts (bound via satisfies readonly Event[]) so the new exhaustiveness test iterates the live Event union rather than a hardcoded array — adding a new Event without updating EVENT_VALUES produces a compile error, and without updating TRANSITIONS or IGNORED_PAIRS causes the test to fail with a clear (State, Event) pair message. Also added user_cancel → abandoned to the bootstrapping state (side effects: run teardown, remove worktree) to close the coverage gap documented in the prior feat-state-machine handoff; this required updating the STATES_WITH_USER_CANCEL set and DOCUMENTED_NON_ABANDONED_PAIRS list in both existing test files, and removing the now-stale 'bootstrapping is missing user_cancel' negative test. Two new test files were created: (1) tests/state-machine/exhaustiveness.test.ts walks STATE_VALUES × EVENT_VALUES and asserts every pair is either defined in TRANSITIONS or explicitly listed in IGNORED_PAIRS (236 entries organized by state with explanatory comments); a second test verifies no IGNORED_PAIRS entry has drifted to be actually defined. (2) tests/state-machine/cancel-from-every-state.test.ts iterates STATE_VALUES, skips the two terminal states (complete, abandoned), and for each non-terminal state asserts user_cancel returns a direct transition to abandoned with the exact sideEffects strings from transitions.ts. All 199 state-machine tests pass. The 2 failing schema tests (workflows column count) are pre-existing failures from the archived_at migration added by feat-workflow-archive, unrelated to this feature.
known_risks: IGNORED_PAIRS in exhaustiveness.test.ts is a static set of 236 string literals. If the TRANSITIONS table grows (new events added to some states), the corresponding pairs must be removed from IGNORED_PAIRS; otherwise the stale-entries test will catch and fail, alerting the developer.

EXPECTED_SIDE_EFFECTS in cancel-from-every-state.test.ts is hardcoded. If transitions.ts changes a user_cancel side-effect string, the test will fail with an exact mismatch — which is the desired behavior, but the developer must update both files in sync.

The bootstrapping user_cancel side-effects (['run teardown', 'remove worktree']) are the author's best judgment for partial-bootstrap cleanup. If the worktree manager does not exist yet when bootstrapping is cancelled, teardown may be a no-op; the pipeline engine must handle that gracefully.

entries.note: Implemented test-real-backend-fixture. Created src/web/e2e/fixtures/realBackend.ts which exports an extended Playwright `test` with a `backend` fixture that yields { baseURL: string, db: DbPool }. The fixture boots startServer({ port: 0, noScheduler: true, _gitCheck: async () => {} }) from src/cli/start.ts against a unique temp .yoke/ directory under os.tmpdir(), then installs page.route('**/api/**', ...) and page.routeWebSocket('**/stream', ...) handlers to proxy all API and WebSocket traffic from the vite-served frontend to the real Fastify backend. Tear-down runs in a finally block: handle.close() then fs.rmSync() of the temp dir. Created src/web/e2e/real/smoke.spec.ts which seeds one workflow via backend.db.writer, navigates to '/', and asserts the workflow name is visible in the sidebar. Updated playwright.config.ts to split into two projects: 'chromium' (testIgnore real/) and 'real-backend' (testMatch real/) so the suites can be run independently. Found and fixed a route.fetch() API bug in the fixture: Playwright's route.fetch() takes a single options object ({url, ...}) not a positional url string; passing the proxyUrl as the first argument caused all proxy requests to silently fetch the original vite URL (returning 404/HTML), making the real backend invisible to the frontend. Fixed by passing { url: proxyUrl, ... } and filtering the host header. All 171 tests pass (164 chromium mocked + 7 real-backend including the new smoke spec).
known_risks: The existing workflow-archive.spec.ts and feature-board-data.spec.ts in e2e/real/ use mocked page.route() calls (not the real backend fixture), so they are not upgraded to use the real backend. They run under the real-backend project but do not exercise the real server. When other teams add real-backend specs, they should use the realBackend fixture.

The fixture's API proxy uses route.fetch() which makes HTTP requests from the Playwright Node.js process to the local backend. If a test needs to assert on specific request headers the browser sends, the proxy may not forward them perfectly (e.g. cookies, CORS headers). For the current smoke test this is not an issue.

The WebSocket proxy via ws.connectToServer() sets up bidirectional forwarding, but Playwright's implementation may not handle all edge cases (e.g. binary frames, close codes). The smoke test only needs the workflow list (REST) not the WebSocket data, so any WS proxy issues don't affect the smoke test result.
The flaky 'Show archived' test (workflow-archive.spec.ts) fails occasionally under high parallel load due to a pre-existing timing issue in that spec (not introduced by this feature).

entries.note: Implemented fix-status-vocabulary in full. Three changes: (1) src/shared/types/workflow.ts now exports WORKFLOW_STATUS_VALUES (an array bound via `as const satisfies readonly WorkflowStatus[]` — adding a new status without updating the array is a TypeScript error) and WORKFLOW_STATUS_LABELS (a Record<WorkflowStatus, string> mapping each status to its display label). (2) WorkflowList.tsx imports both, derives STATUS_OPTIONS as ['all', ...WORKFLOW_STATUS_VALUES], replaces the hardcoded statusChipClass switch/case with a STATUS_CHIP_CLASS lookup object covering all real engine statuses (pending→gray, in_progress→blue, pending_stage_approval→yellow, completed→green, completed_with_blocked→orange, abandoned→red), and uses statusLabel() for option display text instead of naive charAt(0).toUpperCase(). (3) tests/api/workflows-list.test.ts imports WORKFLOW_STATUS_VALUES and adds two new describe blocks: one that iterates the union and asserts the server returns at least one matching row per status filter value, and one that asserts no ?status param returns all statuses. Also created src/web/e2e/real/workflow-list-status-filter.spec.ts with three real-backend tests: per-status filter returns only matching rows (with exact:true to distinguish Status-pending from Status-pending_stage_approval), dropdown renders human-readable labels via WORKFLOW_STATUS_LABELS, and ?status=in_progress URL param on initial load initialises the dropdown correctly. All 8 Vitest workflows-list tests pass; all 15 real-backend Playwright tests pass (3 new + 12 pre-existing).
known_risks: The Playwright spec uses getByText(..., {exact:true}) to avoid strict-mode violations from substring matches (Status-pending vs Status-pending_stage_approval). If workflow names change in structure (e.g. adding a new status whose name is a prefix of another), the exact match correctly handles it.

The STATUS_CHIP_CLASS object falls through to a gray default for unknown statuses. Adding a new WorkflowStatus without updating STATUS_CHIP_CLASS does NOT produce a TypeScript error — it falls to the default. This is intentional to avoid crashing on unexpected values, but means new statuses appear gray until STATUS_CHIP_CLASS is updated.
WORKFLOW_STATUS_LABELS is typed as Record<WorkflowStatus, string>, so adding a new status without updating WORKFLOW_STATUS_LABELS IS a TypeScript error — this satisfies RC-2.

entries.note: Implemented infra-contract-script in full. Three deliverables: (1) docs/design/schemas/api-responses.schema.json — JSON Schema 2020-12 with $defs for WorkflowsListResponse, TimelineResponse, UsageResponse, TimeseriesResponse, ItemsDataResponse (opaque), ItemSessionsResponse, and SessionLogResponse. All response shapes use camelCase field names and additionalProperties: false so snake_case drift causes immediate schema rejection. WorkflowStatus is defined as an enum $def referenced by WorkflowRow. (2) scripts/check-api-contract.js — run via `pnpm test:contract` (tsx scripts/check-api-contract.js). Boots startServer({ port: 0, noScheduler: true, _gitCheck: async () => {} }) against a temp .yoke/ dir, seeds one workflow/item/session via db.writer, fetches all 7 documented endpoints, validates each with Ajv 2020-12, and reports FAIL <endpoint> + specific field path on drift. Tears down server and temp dir in both normal and signal (SIGINT/SIGTERM) paths. Runs in under 10 seconds. (3) tests/api/contract-schema.test.ts — 17 Vitest tests covering: WorkflowStatus enum matches WORKFLOW_STATUS_VALUES runtime array; WorkflowRow accepts valid TypeScript-typed data; WorkflowRow rejects snake_case fields; WorkflowRow rejects unknown status values; ItemSession requires camelCase fields; TimelineResponse requires workflowId (not workflow_id); UsageRow requires camelCase token fields; SessionLogResponse entries are strings (raw JSONL) not objects. All 17 tests pass. pnpm test:contract exits 0 with 7/7 OK. No CI infrastructure exists in the repo so the 'separate CI job' AC is deferred.
deferred_criteria: AC: Wire as a separate CI job — no .github/workflows/ directory exists in the repository; no CI infrastructure to wire to. The pnpm test:contract script is ready to be added to a CI job when CI is set up.
known_risks: The contract script uses the global fetch() API (Node 18+). If this project runs on Node 16 or earlier, fetch is not available and the script will fail with ReferenceError. Mitigation: tsx and modern ESM usage strongly imply Node 18+.

ItemsDataResponse is validated only as 'parseable JSON' (not against a fixed schema), since items.data is opaque. If a future endpoint change returns a non-JSON body for this path, the contract check would catch it via the HTTP 200 + parse check but wouldn't catch shape drift within the JSON object.

The schema uses additionalProperties: false on response envelopes (WorkflowsListResponse, TimelineResponse, etc.). If a new field is added to a response without updating the schema, the contract check exits 1. This is intentional — it prevents silent envelope expansion — but the developer must update both the API and the schema together.

TimelineEvent does not use additionalProperties: false (it has an open 'extra' field that can be any JSON value). If the event schema becomes stricter in the future, this $def should be updated accordingly.

feature-board.spec.ts A5 test uses page.waitForTimeout(500) to wait for rogue re-renders. known_risks: If an item races to terminal between the non-terminal SELECT and applyItemTransition, cancelledCount still increments but no item.state frame is emitted (the newState !== 'abandoned' guard handles this). The cancelledItems count in the HTTP response may therefore be higher than the number of item.state frames — acceptable since the race window is narrow under SQLite serialized writes.

entries.note: Implemented r2-09 push-branch in full. Created src/server/github/push.ts exporting pushBranch(branchName, worktreePath, deps: { execGit }): Promise<PushResult>. The function runs `git -C <worktreePath> push -u origin <branchName>` via injected execGit and classifies errors by stderr substring: auth_failed ('authentication', 'permission denied', 'could not read username', 'could not read password'), non_fast_forward ('non-fast-forward', 'updates were rejected', 'fetch first'), network_failed ('could not resolve', 'network is unreachable', 'timeout', 'connection refused'), other (anything else). Returns {ok:true} on success or {ok:false, kind, message, rawStderr} on failure. No --force or --no-verify flags are ever passed. Also exports makeProductionPushDeps() keeping child_process out of test imports. Created tests/github/push.test.ts with 15 tests covering: success path, correct args shape (includes -C and -u, excludes --force), all four auth_failed markers (4 cases), all four non_fast_forward markers (3 cases), all four network_failed markers (4 cases), other classification, and rawStderr field inclusion. All 1280 Vitest tests pass; typecheck exits 0 for both root and web packages.
known_risks: makeProductionPushDeps() passes the full error stderr as the Error.message, so classifyStderr operates on that string. If the production git stderr includes a prefix (like 'git push failed (128): ...') before the actual stderr text, classification still works because it uses includes() not startsWith(). However if execFile wraps the stderr in a different format, the test stubs and production may diverge.

The args AC-4 grep check counts the message string 'git push failed: kind' as the only 'git push' string in src/ — this is a human-readable message label, not a command invocation, so the AC is satisfied.

entries.note: Implemented r2-07 fix-control-matrix-rules in full. The error field serializes GithubAuthError to 'GITHUB_TOKEN: <reason>; gh_auth: <reason>' and GithubApiError to error.message. If UI consumers expect a structured object, the string representation may be insufficient — but GithubButton.tsx uses it as a tooltip string, so this is correct.

entries.note: Implemented r2-08 in full. (1) Created src/server/github/remote-parse.ts with parseGitRemoteUrl(), a pure function that parses SSH (git@host:owner/repo.git) and HTTPS (https://host/owner/repo.git) remote URLs into owner/repo — handles with/without .git, trailing slash, case-insensitive host, returns null for malformed URLs. (2) Updated src/server/scheduler/ingest.ts: added IngestDeps interface with getRemoteOriginUrl(configDir) and makeProductionIngestDeps() factory (uses execFileSync, reads local git config only, never contacts origin). ingestWorkflow now resolves the remote URL via deps before the transaction, then calls initGithubState inside the same db.transaction as the workflow INSERT (better-sqlite3 handles the nested call as a SAVEPOINT). Resume path (isResume=true) early-returns before the transaction, so initGithubState is never called on resume. (3) Updated src/server/scheduler/scheduler.ts to pass makeProductionIngestDeps() to ingestWorkflow. (4) Created tests/github/remote-parse.test.ts with 23 unit tests covering all URL formats. (5) Expanded tests/scheduler/ingest.test.ts with 6 new tests covering: disabled (github.enabled=false), unconfigured (github.enabled=true, no remote), idle (github.enabled=true, valid remote), unconfigured when deps omitted, unconfigured when remote URL malformed, resume path preserves github_state. All 1428 Vitest tests pass, typecheck clean.
known_risks: initGithubState is called from inside db.transaction() which itself calls db.transaction() internally — better-sqlite3 handles this via SAVEPOINT semantics. If the nested call ever throws, the outer transaction rolls back entirely (correct behaviour), but the error message may reference the savepoint rather than the outer transaction.

makeProductionIngestDeps uses execFileSync with a 5-second timeout. On a system under heavy I/O load, `git remote get-url origin` could theoretically time out, causing github_state=unconfigured for a workflow that is actually configured. This is safe (unconfigured is a valid initial state) but could surprise users who configured github.enabled=true.

The static import of execFileSync from node:child_process at the top of ingest.ts means the child_process module is loaded whenever ingest.ts is imported, including in tests. This is a Node.js built-in with no side effects, so it does not affect test isolation.

known_risks: prompt_assembly_failed now uses session_fail+permanent which transitions the item to awaiting_user (not awaiting_retry). This is intentional — prompt assembly failures are not transient; the user must intervene. If the classification policy changes, the guardCtx parameter should be updated.

The backoff_elapsed test relies on DEFAULT_RETRY_LADDER[2] = 'awaiting_user' being the exhaustion sentinel. If the default ladder changes, the test must be updated to use a retry_count that hits the new sentinel position.

entries.note: Implemented r2-10 (auto-PR on workflow complete). Extended Scheduler._handleStageComplete to call _triggerAutoPr(workflowId) when the final stage completes with a terminal status and config.github.enabled + config.github.auto_pr are set. _triggerAutoPr runs asynchronously via an injected asyncRunner (default fire-and-forget), reads workflow branch_name/worktree_path, resolves owner/repo from git remote, gathers last 10 commit subjects and last non-harness handoff note, builds the PR body via buildPrBody, pushes the branch, then calls createPr. Push failures and PR errors write github_state='failed' without retrying or throwing. Added: (1) src/server/github/pr-body.ts — pure buildPrBody function; (2) src/server/github/service.ts — renamed _writeGithubState → writeGithubState (export) so the scheduler can call it; (3) src/server/scheduler/scheduler.ts — AutoPrDeps interface, autoPr option, _triggerAutoPr and _makeGithubBroadcast methods, _handleStageComplete extension; (4) tests/github/pr-body.test.ts — 13 unit tests for the pure function; (5) tests/scheduler/auto-pr-on-complete.test.ts — 10 integration tests covering all 9 ACs using real SQLite + injected stubs. All 23 new tests pass; 14 pre-existing test failures are unrelated (github-create-pr API endpoint and AttentionBanner KIND_MAP); typecheck shows 4 pre-existing errors only.
known_risks: Production defaults for getOwnerRepo, getRecentCommits, getLastHandoffNote, and push use dynamic import() so they do not inflate the main bundle. If any of these modules fail to load at runtime, _triggerAutoPr catches and writes github_state='failed'.

The createPr production default is an inline closure inside _triggerAutoPr so it can capture this.db. It also re-imports auth and adapter modules dynamically. If the import paths change, a runtime error will be caught and written as a failed state.

getLastHandoffNote reads handoff.json from configDir; if the file is absent or malformed, it returns null (no handoff note section in PR body). This is intentional — a missing handoff file should not prevent PR creation.

entries.note: Implemented r2-11 (manual Create PR flow). Added POST /api/workflows/:id/github/create-pr in server.ts following the RC-3 injection pattern: delegated to CreatePrExecutorFn, idempotent via IdempotencyStore (commandId). Added createPrExecutor to ServerCallbacks. Created create-pr-executor.ts with writer:BetterSqlite3.Database (not db.reader()) for WAL-safe validation reads. Wired production createPrFn in start.ts (inline: read workflow, resolve owner/repo, push branch, call createPr). Created githubButtonRules.ts (shouldShowCreatePrButton pure function), updated GithubButton.tsx with workflowId/workflowStatus props and Create PR button for idle/failed states. Updated WorkflowDetailRoute.tsx. Fixed two pre-existing issues: KIND_MAP export in engine.ts and type cast for customAttentionPayload. All 35 new tests pass (13 API, 17 visibility, 5 attentionBanner), typecheck clean.
known_risks: The production createPrFn in start.ts is inlined (~70 lines) rather than extracted to a shared helper module. It mirrors _triggerAutoPr but is not DRY. Acceptable for now since the two paths (auto-PR on completion vs manual trigger) have slightly different error handling needs.

entries.note: r2-07: Fixed ControlMatrix Retry button to POST /api/workflows/:id/retry instead of sending a WS control frame (which the control executor rejects as invalid_action). Modified WorkflowDetailRoute.tsx sendControl callback to branch on action===retry and use fetch(). Added retry-control-button.spec.ts real-backend Playwright test. All 1498 unit tests pass, typecheck clean.
known_risks: Playwright e2e test not yet run against live backend — exercises real scheduler retry path

entries.note: Addressed both AC-3 blocking issues from review attempt 2. (1) parallel-items-stream.spec.ts no longer seeds sessions in the DB — seedParallelWorkflow now inserts only workflow + items rows, so workflow.snapshot has empty activeSessions and buildFromSnapshot produces an empty map. After items appear on screen, session.started is broadcast for each item (A, B, C) via backend.broadcast(), exercising the upsert path in the session.started WS handler of WorkflowDetailRoute. (2) The test now clicks item A a second time after switching to item B, asserting that A's stream content is still visible (itemActiveSession map preserved A's entry since no session.ended was broadcast for A). All 1498 Vitest tests pass; typecheck clean.
known_risks: The session.started broadcasts are sent immediately after items become visible on screen. There is no explicit UI indicator that the session.started frames have been processed before the stream.text broadcasts are sent. The 5s timeout on getByText assertions provides sufficient headroom in practice — WS frames are processed in order and the React setState batching resolves before the Playwright assertion fires.

The test relies on the render store being a singleton that retains text deltas for a session even when a different item is selected. If the render store is ever scoped per-item (reset on item deselect), this test would need to re-broadcast text after each click.

entries.note: Completed r2-06 seed_failed error path. Added seed_failed to Event union/EVENT_VALUES; added (ready,seed_failed) and (in_progress,seed_failed) transitions to TRANSITIONS table; updated engine.ts PENDING_ATTENTION_LABELS with seed_failed kind, added customAttentionPayload field to GuardContext, and tightened applyPendingSideEffects type annotation to fix TS error; wired scheduler.ts ready-case seeding failure to fire seed_failed transition with customAttentionPayload containing message+stage+item_id, and in_progress case to detect per-item placeholder after user_retry and re-seed; added buildAttentionMessage case for seed_failed; updated exhaustiveness.test.ts IGNORED_PAIRS for all states without seed_failed transitions; created tests/scheduler/per-item-seeder-error.test.ts (9 tests, AC-1–4: attention insertion, notice broadcast, tight-loop prevention, user_retry re-seeding); created src/web/e2e/real/seed-failed-banner.spec.ts (2 Playwright tests asserting banner renders within 2s with kind=seed_failed). typecheck clean, 1498 tests pass.
known_risks: seed-failed-banner.spec.ts uses backend.broadcast() injection (not the real scheduler tick) — the full end-to-end path with a real scheduler tick is covered by unit tests, not Playwright.

blocking_issues: AC-3: scheduler.ts:1414-1419 broadcasts session.started WITHOUT itemId — the payload only contains {sessionId, phase, attempt, startedAt}. Because the client handler guards on `if (p.itemId)` (WorkflowDetailRoute.tsx:249), the upsert path is dead code in production: no real per-item session started by the scheduler will ever populate itemActiveSession after the initial snapshot. The E2E spec (parallel-items-stream.spec.ts:72-80) works around this by using backend.broadcast() with a hand-crafted payload that manually includes itemId. The test proves the client upsert logic is correct but does not prove the scheduler emits itemId. The feature spec explicitly requires 'maintain it via session.started (upsert)' — that path never fires in a real workflow.
non_blocking: RC-2: removeBySessionId (itemSessionMap.ts:57-72) is O(n) per session.ended frame. Acceptable since session.ended is infrequent and the map is bounded by concurrent item count (< 20). High-frequency stream.text frames do not trigger this path.

known_risks: retry-control-button.spec.ts seeds a session with status='in_progress' directly in the DB so ControlMatrix renders for the selected item. If ControlMatrix's session-visibility logic changes, the seeding must be updated.

The {retried:true} patch handling in WorkflowDetailRoute.tsx cycles unsubscribe+subscribe. If the server doesn't send a fresh snapshot within the Playwright timeout (3s), the item status assertion may flake on very slow CI.

The workflowStatus in ControlCtx is typed as string (not WorkflowStatus) to match the snapshot projection.
